title: anthemis triumfetti subsp. khorasanica
intro: Anthemis triumfetti subsp. khorasanica, also known as Khorasan chamomile, is a subspecies of the Anthemis triumfetti plant, belonging to the daisy family and native to Iran and Afghanistan. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Khorasan chamomile include reducing anxiety and insomnia, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, due to its high concentration of apigenin and other bioactive compounds. Khorasan chamomile can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support overall well-being.
